publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and runnable core task for Recursive functions; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Recursive functions: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Any function that calls itself will eventually stop, and tail recursion uses constant stack space in Python.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. State the base case and a strictly smaller progress measure, and choose iteration when depth can exceed Python's recursion limit.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. State the base case and a strictly smaller progress measure, and choose iteration when depth can exceed Python's recursion limit.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

items.title: Recursive functions: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read a non-negative integer n. Implement recursive factorial with an explicit base case and print the result.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Python: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.explanation: Every recursive call decreases the non-negative integer toward the zero base case.
